explanation: The property specifies that if there is an information request (infoRequest), then eventually the robot will provide its current location and also return where measurements were taken from. This captures the natural language as it emphasizes understanding where the robot has been in an area and where it took measurements from.

Whenever an information request is made (infoRequest), the robot should eventually explain the locations it has visited and where it took measurements from. This aligns with the intention of understanding the robot's path and the measurement origins.

Globally, whenever there is an information request (infoRequest), the robot must eventually explain the path it has taken and where it took measurements from. This matches the natural language goal of understanding past locations and measurement points.

If there is an information request (infoRequest), then the robot must at some point confirm and elaborate on past travel areas and pertinent locations where measurements were taken. This captures the expectation of knowledge sharing about areas visited and sampled.

Every time the robot receives an information request (infoRequest), it is required to indicate its whereabouts and the origins of its measurements, thus fulfilling the inquiry about past traversed areas and measurement origins.

An information request (infoRequest) triggers the robot to communicate its current location and any additional areas visited from which measurements were taken, aligning with understanding the robot's movements and data collection points.

If the system has been tasked with an information request (infoRequest), the robot provides an understanding of the regions it has visited and the sources of its measurement data, matching the requirement for knowledge on past activities and data origins.

Upon receiving an information request (infoRequest), the robot must elucidate on the intents of its planned path and former measurement locales, ensuring comprehension of its navigational and sampling history.

When prompted by an information request (infoRequest), it is mandatory for the robot to produce details about both its current location and return the origins of its measurements to satisfy the understanding of its spatial-temporal activities.

Globally, under an information request (infoRequest), the system ensures the explanation of taken measurements and the visited locations, corresponding to the desire for insight into the paths and areas from where data has been retrieved.
The LTL captures the requirement that eventually, the robot should provide detailed explanations about the locations it has visited and where it took measurements from.
The LTL property states that globally, if information is requested, the robot should explain its location and the origins of its measurements.
This LTL expression ensures that globally, when explaining its location is required, the robot should provide information about the path it currently takes and the measurements done.
Eventually, the system returns detailed information about the robot's location and where it took measurements.
The robot is required to eventually provide an explanation of the path it has been following and the origin of the measurements taken along the way.
Globally, whenever a detailed response is required, the robot should provide detailed information about visited locations and measurements taken.
Globally, if an explanation is requested, the robot should return information about the locations it has visited and associated measurement origins in a detailed manner.
The robot must either explain the current or intended path in detail, whenever detailed information or return of intended path is requested.
The system's requirement specifies that eventually, the robot should explain where measurements were taken from and its current location in detail.
Globally, if an explanation is demanded, providing locations visited and origins of measurements should serve as a valid response.
Globally, if an explanation is requested, the robot quickly needs to provide its current location and the locations it has visited.
The explanation states that globally, if a detailed report is requested, the robot should provide its current location and explain the measurements from the locations visited.
The LTL property ensures that eventually, the robot provides detailed information about the locations it has visited and where it took measurements, fulfilling the information request.
Globally, whenever a detailed response is requested, information about the path the robot has been following and measurements taken is provided.
The requirement ensures that globally, if the robot is to provide explanations, it must be about visited locations and measurement taking in detailed terms.
The robot should eventually provide a detailed account of where it thinks it has been and provide explanations for all measurements taken during its mission.
Globally, if an explanation is requested, then information about the places visited and the layout of these locations must be explained.
Globally, whenever there is a request for information, the robot should explain the visited sites and the places where measurements were done.
Eventually, the robot should provide a detailed explanation of its current location as well as where measurements were taken.
Globally, it is expected that when detailed location information is requested, the robot provides it along with measurement origin information.
The LTL rule ensures that when providing an explanation, the robot should address both the locations it has visited and the paths taken with details.
There is an obligation for the robot to eventually disclose where it has visited and taken measurements with a detailed account, addressing any information requests.
When asked for detailed information, the robot should explain both the path it took and where measurements were taken, globally.
Globally, it is expected that requests for explanations about visiting locations and collecting measurements should be met with a detailed account.
The rule requires eventually giving a detailed explanation of where the robot has visited and measurement origins, especially when detailed information is needed.
Globally, any request for detail mandates that the robot explains the locations where measurements were made, along with the current location data, in depth.
Whenever there is a request for explanation, globally, it must cover both visited places and the actual measurements obtained from these spots.
Ensuring that globally, whenever detailed data is requested, the robot focuses on explaining both its path and origin of its readings.
The requirement sets that eventually, when detail is asked for, returning visited places and measurement origins should be met comprehensively by the robot.
Globally ensures responses to detailed requests about mission visits and measurement origins are thoroughly explained.
Demands a detailed explanation about visited locations and measurements from visited spots, whenever an information request is made, globally.
LTL specification dictates that eventually, the system should explain measurements taken and the location data in a detailed manner.
The robot globally ensures a detailed account of its visits and the measurement origins in response to explain requests.
Globally, whenever explanations are required, comprehensive data on all places visited and related measurements must be handled.
If the situation involves providing deep explanations, the robot has to address all visited locations and where data was measured, consistently over time.
This expression guarantees, that globally, explanations on measurement taking and visited sites are delivered with utmost detail.
LTL mandates the robot to ensure whenever detail is asked, visited locations and data origin are provided comprehensively.
Explains how within all paths taken, measurements taken need to be disclosed comprehensively fulfilling detailed responses.
Whenever missions are conducted, details about visited areas and measured values from these should be communicated eventually.
This LTL condition maintains globally held beliefs where explanations of mission path and outcomes should be rendered with sufficient details.
